name: Barbell Standing Calf Raise
resistance_profile: Free barbell on the back loads the gastrocnemius through a full stretch; balance caps the usable load.
cues: Brace the core and keep the bar stable
Full stretch at the bottom, full rise at the top
Keep the knees straight for the gastrocnemius
execution_steps: Set a barbell across your upper back as for a squat and step the balls of your feet onto a block or plate.
Stand tall with heels hanging off the edge and knees straight.
Lower your heels below the block into a full calf stretch.
Rise onto the balls of your feet to full plantarflexion.
Pause at the top, then lower under control.
good_when: You have a barbell but no calf machine
You want a standing gastrocnemius exercise you can load progressively
bad_when: Balancing the bar limits you before your calves are challenged
You lack a rack or block to set up safely
common_errors: Bouncing to keep balance instead of controlling the rep
Short range of motion at both ends
